operation_candidates.name: Decision matrix lookup by (hardware, workload)
operation_candidates.trigger: User asks 'what should we serve X on?' and supplies hardware + workload constraints.
action: Apply Step-1 hard filters (no NVIDIA -> drop TensorRT-LLM; OSS-only -> drop TensorRT-LLM/TGI; etc.).
Apply Step-2 workload-to-strength mapping (shared-prefix -> SGLang; throughput+NVIDIA+budget -> TensorRT-LLM; default -> vLLM).
Sanity-check hardware topology (PCIe-only -> avoid large TP).
Return primary engine + fallback + the constraint vector that justified the pick.
operation_candidates.output: Primary engine choice, fallback choice, constraint vector justifying both.

operation_candidates.name: Detect wrong-tool-for-the-job symptoms
operation_candidates.trigger: Team is using engine E but reports low throughput, unsupported model, structured-output workarounds, or single-user pain on a multi-user engine.
action: Map symptom to engine mismatch (vLLM stuck on PCIe-TP=4 -> switch to TP=2 + replicas or PP; vLLM with heavy guided_decoding -> A/B SGLang; TGI deployment -> plan migration; Ollama at >=10 concurrent users -> graduate to vLLM; llama.cpp serving many users -> graduate to vLLM).
Produce migration recommendation with cited reason.

operation_candidates.name: Quantization-driven engine pick
operation_candidates.trigger: Team has weights in a specific quantization format already (GGUF / AWQ / GPTQ / FP8 / MLX).
action: GGUF only -> llama.cpp / Ollama / LM Studio (vLLM GGUF is experimental).
AWQ / GPTQ -> vLLM (first-class), SGLang (good), TensorRT-LLM (engine rebuild required).
FP8 on Hopper/Ada+ -> vLLM, SGLang, TensorRT-LLM all support; near-lossless.
MLX format -> MLX / mlx-lm only.
operation_candidates.output: Engine shortlist gated by available weights format.

operation_candidates.name: Structured-output workload routing
operation_candidates.trigger: >=30% of requests need JSON / regex / grammar-constrained output, or the project is an agent platform with tool-call state machines.
action: Default to SGLang (RadixAttention + native state machines beat vLLM guided_decoding at scale).
If staying on vLLM, plan for throughput hit on constrained requests; consider xgrammar backend.
If model not in SGLang's coverage, fall back to vLLM and tune.
operation_candidates.output: SGLang as primary; vLLM as fallback if model coverage gaps.

operation_candidates.name: Apple Silicon / edge routing
operation_candidates.trigger: Target platform is M-series Mac, iPhone/iPad, Jetson, Raspberry Pi, or other ARM/edge.
action: M-series Mac, dev: Ollama (best UX) or MLX (best perf).
M-series Mac, production server: MLX or llama.cpp Metal. Do NOT ship vLLM Metal/MPS (experimental).
Jetson / ARM Linux: llama.cpp, or TensorRT-LLM if Jetson Orin.
CPU-only x86 server: llama.cpp with AVX-512; single-digit users only.
operation_candidates.output: Apple/edge-specific engine pick with required weights format (GGUF or MLX).

operation_candidates.name: Multi-tier deployment design
operation_candidates.trigger: Project has both interactive (premium, latency-bound) and batch (free, throughput-bound) tiers.
action: Premium / interactive: TensorRT-LLM (if NVIDIA-locked + budget) OR vLLM FP8 + prefix caching.
Free / batch: vLLM AWQ-4 (high concurrency, lower cost) OR SGLang for prefix-sharing.
Dev / staging: Ollama (fast model swaps).
Edge / on-device client: llama.cpp (GGUF).
Front the whole thing with a single OpenAI-compatible gateway.
operation_candidates.output: Heterogeneous 2-3 engine plan with one API gateway.

operation_candidates.name: Bench-then-pick when the choice is close
operation_candidates.trigger: Two engines both pass hard filters and workload mapping; team disagrees on which to pick.
action: Run a 1-day benchmark with replayed traffic.
Measure P50+P99 TTFT, P50+P99 ITL, tokens/s/GPU, $/M-tokens, peak memory, model-load time.
Use real production ISL/OSL; do NOT use MMLU-style synthetic prompts.
Document the constraint vector + numbers + the threshold that would flip the decision.
operation_candidates.output: Decision memo with constraint vector, measured numbers, decision-flip threshold.

operation_candidates.name: License / compliance filter
operation_candidates.trigger: Procurement, security, or legal review of the inference path.
action: TensorRT-LLM = NVIDIA proprietary -> eliminate if shop forbids closed-source on the inference path.
TGI = HFOIL Apache-restricted -> check commercial-use clauses for some derived models.
vLLM / SGLang / llama.cpp / Ollama / MLX = Apache/MIT -> permissive.
